createpropulsion.ponder.shared.redstone_: Input on the opposite side decreases shift.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.burner.ac: Once burning, connected heat consumers can start operating.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.solid_bur: Solid Burners support a redstone thermostat override.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.redstone_: Input on one side increases transmission shift.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.liquid_bu: Liquid Burners also support thermostat redstone control.
Liquid fuel properties affect heat sustain and performance.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.burner.co: With thermostat override, fuel is consumed continuously.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.redstone_: Full signal gives full configured transmission output.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.liquid_bu: Liquid Burners can be chained with adjacent systems.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.burner.ef: Maintain good fuel and setup to keep burner efficiency high.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.liquid_bu: Liquid Burners consume valid liquid fuels to produce heat.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.redstone_: Incremental mode changes shift level step-by-step.
Direct mode maps input signal directly to output ratio.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.tilt_adap: Tilt Adapter converts redstone differential into a target output angle.
Equal left and right signal keeps the adapter centered.
Higher left signal tilts output in one direction.
Higher right signal tilts output in the opposite direction.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.solid_bur: Solid Burners can generate heat for compatible machines above them.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.stirling_: A Stirling Engine runs when heated from below.
Heat from below makes the engine generate rotational force.
Without heat, the engine stops producing speed.
Powering the clutch stops the pump, fuel flow stops, and the engine winds down.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.liquid_bu: They can pass fluid through while operating as burners.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.redstone_: Use the value panel to switch control modes.
Stronger side wins and shifts output accordingly.
If both sides are powered, net change depends on signal difference.
Output scales proportionally with received redstone strength.
createpropulsion.thruster.tooltip.summar: Provides directional thrust when powered by redstone and fueled with Turpentine.
createpropulsion.redstone_converter.tool: Converts redstone signal strength into a configurable redstone output signal.
createpropulsion.creative_thruster.toolt: Infinite-fuel thruster with configurable output and particle mode cycling via wrench.
createpropulsion.ion_thruster.tooltip.su: Provides directional thrust when powered by redstone and supplied with FE energy.
createpropulsion.vector_thruster.tooltip: Directional thruster that can also yaw and pitch for vectored thrust control.
Directional thruster that can yaw and pitch using liquid fuel instead of FE energy.
createpropulsion.copycat_wing.tooltip.su: A lightweight copycat sail block for contraptions. Place and then apply another block to copy its material.
A wider copycat sail block for contraptions. Place and then apply another block to copy its material.
createpropulsion.copycat_wing_12.tooltip: The widest copycat sail block for contraptions. Place and then apply another block to copy its material.
createpropulsion.turpentine_bucket.toolt: Fuel for Thrusters. Hold Shift to see thrust and burn rate summary.
createpropulsion.coral_bucket.tooltip.su: Fuel for Coral Generator. Hold Shift to see conversion rate.
createpropulsion.oxidizer_bucket.tooltip: High-energy fluid used to stabilize and power large multiblock thrusters.
createpropulsion.tilt_adapter.tooltip.su: A device that tilts rotational output based on analog redstone signal.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.thruster_: Thrusters accept configured fuels through connected pipes.
Invalid fluids do not power the thruster.
Use valid thruster fuels in the input line.
Now apply redstone power so the thruster actually produces thrust.
2x2 multiblocks only accept fluid from the rear intake side.
A 2x2x2 multiblock is built from 8 normal thrusters.
First line (no clutch) is invalid here: it is not pumping into the rear intake.
Because this line does not feed the rear tanks, the thruster does nothing.
You can only feed from the rear side for multiblocks.
Remove the invalid top line and feed from the rear side instead.
You can only feed from the rear side for multiblocks.
With the valid rear-fed line, insert proper thruster fuel.
Finally apply redstone to the thruster: now it actually runs.
3x3 multiblocks follow the same rear-intake-only rule.
A 3x3x3 multiblock is built from 27 normal thrusters.
You can only feed from the rear side for multiblocks.
Feed the back layer input manifold for full operation.
Insert valid thruster fuel into the rear-fed manifold.
Apply redstone to the thruster and the whole multiblock works.
createpropulsion.ponder.shared.vector_th: Vector Thruster uses Forge energy to produce thrust.
Forge energy is stored in the thruster's internal energy storage.
Currently %s FE is able to be stored in the thruster's internal storage.
Generators can power from below, this can be anything that uses forge energy system.
Vector Thrusters can rotate in all directions, this is controlled by redstone signals directly embedded into the thruster itself.
A redstone power can be anything from 0-15, this will increase the distance of rotation from the base.
Each side except front and back has a redstone signal place, that direction is where it will be rotated towards when that signal is powered.
Powering a thruster is easy, any external signal to the thruster will be the power, this can range from 0-15, and will use power accordingly.
Creative Vector Thrusters produce configurable thrust without fuel or Forge energy.
Set the creative thrust amount from the value box on the bottom of the thruster.
Creative Vector Thrusters still use redstone power as their throttle, from 0-15.
Liquid Vector Thrusters vector their thrust like normal vector thrusters, but run on pumped liquid fuel.
Pump valid thruster fuel into the liquid vector thruster through the pipe below.
Once fuel is pumped in, the internal tank can feed the thruster while it is powered.
Apply redstone power to throttle the liquid vector thruster, from 0-15.
createpropulsion.creative_vector_thruste: Vector thruster with configurable creative thrust and wrench-cycled particle mode.
createpropulsion.coral_generator.tooltip: Converts Coral fluid into FE and outputs power from the top and bottom faces.
createpropulsion.platinum_fluid_tank.too: Stores fluids like a Copper Fluid Tank, but with doubled capacity per block.
item.createpropulsion.assembler_stick.to: Quickly _assemble_ or _disassemble_ contraptions by right-clicking blocks.
Automatically creates one _honey glue box_ over the connected structure.
Left-click to _remove_ honey glue touching the block.
item.createpropulsion.glued_contraption_: Captures a glued contraption into Create placement mode and then _moves_ it.
Source contraption is _hard-wiped_ and tool is restored.
Captures a glued contraption into Create placement mode and _clones_ it.
Original remains; schematic is _consumed_ and tool restored.
item.createpropulsion.contraption_remove: Hard wipes the full connected glued contraption instantly.
Right-click to _hard-wipe_ all connected glued blocks and glue entities.
message.assemblystick.disassemble_failed: Disassembly failed due to safety checks.
message.assemblystick.auto_glue_too_larg: Auto Glue stopped: more than %s blocks connected.
message.assemblystick.mover_create_missi: Create is required for Glued Contraption Mover.
message.assemblystick.mover_too_large: Move failed: more than %s connected blocks.
message.assemblystick.mover_prepared: Contraption move prepared. Use Create schematic placement to place.
message.assemblystick.mover_finalize_fai: Placement finished, but source cleanup failed.
